snippet.title: Interview with the Rt  Hon  Michael Manley ON OCC Part 3/4
snippet.description:
Personally Speaking with Donette Chin-Loy. Produced by Frame by Frame Productions and Donette Chin-Loy. 

The last full length interview with former, late Prime Minister of Jamaica, Rt. Hon. Michael Norman Manley ON OCC.

Michael Norman Manley ON OCC (10 December 1924 -- 6 March 1997) was a Jamaican politician who served as the fourth Prime Minister of Jamaica from 1972 to 1980 and from 1989 to 1992. Coming from a prosperous background, Manley was a democratic socialist. He is considered one of Jamaica's best Prime Ministers since independence, according to public opinion polls.

The second son of Jamaica's Premier Norman Washington Manley and Jamaican artist Edna Manley, Michael Manley was a charismatic figure who became the leader of the Jamaican People's National Party a few months before his father's death in 1969...

snippet.title: FAE  ELLINGTON'S INTERVIEW WITH FORMER PRIME MINISTER OF JAMAICA, MICHAEL MANLEY... PART 2 VIDEO
snippet.description:
This interview with the late Michael Manley, former Prime Minister of Jamaica was recorded in 1995... 28 years ago. It had its first and only airing on JBC Radio One, on the now defunct Jamaica Broadcasting Corporation. I have decided to publish the conversation in seven parts, beginning on Sunday, September 10 with the final part to be published on Saturday, September 16, which will be the anniversary of my 49th year in broadcast media.

In some parts you'll hear the voices of Mr. Manley's wife, Glynne and that of programme musician, Marjorie Whylie. 

28 years ago broadcast engineer was Peter Brown. 
Thanks to Dadrian HoSang, Sound Engineer at Creative Sounds.
Special thanks to Damion Mitchell.

Pictures were provided by -
(c) The Gleaner Company (Media) Limited 
Mrs. Glynne Manley
Miss Sarah Manley
Mr. Granville Valentine, National Workers' Union
Michael Manley Foundation

I am certain that this interview in its 7 parts will provide new information and insights, generate discussion and passionate debate, and perhaps it will trigger ideas for further, reading, research and investigation.

Thanks for listening. Please share and comment.

Walk good.

Fae

snippet.title: Truth to be Told: Michael Manley in Conversation by Glynne Manley
snippet.description:
Michael Manley was Jamaica's Prime Minister between 1972 and 1980 when his government attempted a series of decolonizing reforms. He was defeated in a violent election in 1980 and the Washington consensus was ushered in. He returned to power in 1989 taking a more moderate stance. Manley's leadership style and legacy is heatedly contested, but does it have any relevance for the present moment? Truth be Told comprises three years of candid interviews with Michael Manley conducted by his wife Glynne leading up to his death in 1997.  Now, in the context of a major shift in the balance of power in the hemisphere, leading scholars of the Caribbean, Canada, the US and Jamaica take up the book in relation to Manley's legacy. They do so through the lens of contemporary issues related to political leadership, the informal economy, gender, mining and extraction, global racism and inequality; and land and labour rights.

Join the conversation with:

Anthony Bogues, Asa Messer Professor of Humanities and Critical Theory, Professor of Africana Studies, and Director of the Center for the Study of Slavery and Justice, Brown University

Kevin Edmonds, Assistant Professor, Caribbean Studies, University of Toronto

Glynne Manley, Interviewer and Editor Truth be Told

Maziki Thame, Professor, Institute of Development Studies, University of the West Indies Mona, Jamaica.

Anna Zalik, Associate Professor, Political Economy of Development, Faculty of Environmental Urban Change at York University

snippet.title: PART 4 FAE A  ELLINGTON'S INTERVIEW WITH MICHAEL MANLEY VIDEO TRADE UNIONISM
snippet.description:
This interview with the late Michael Manley, former Prime Minister of Jamaica was recorded in 1995... 28 years ago. It had it first and only airing on JBC Radio One, on the now defunct Jamaica Broadcasting Corporation. 

I have decided to publish the conversation in seven parts, beginning on Sunday, September 10 with the final part to be published on Saturday, September 16, which will be the anniversary of my 49th year in broadcast media. 

In some parts you'll hear the voices of Mr. Manley's wife, Glynne and that of programme musician, Marjorie Whylie. 
28 years ago broadcast engineer was Peter Brown. 

Thanks to Dadrian HoSang, Sound Engineer at Creative Sounds. 

Special thanks to Damion Mitchell. 

Pictures were provided by - (c) The Gleaner Company (Media) Limited 
Mrs. Glynne Manley 
Miss Sarah Manley 
Mr. Granville Valentine, National Workers' Union 
Michael Manley Foundation 

I am certain that this interview in its 7 parts will provide new information and insights, generate discussion and passionate debate, and perhaps it will trigger ideas for further, reading, research and investigation. 

Thanks for listening. Please share and comment. 

Walk good. 
Fae

I have decided to publish the conversation in seven parts, beginning on Sunday, September 10 with the final part to be published on Saturday, September 16, which will be the anniversary of my 49th year in broadcast media.

In some parts you'll hear the voices of Mr. Manley's wife, Glynne and that of programme musician, Marjorie Whylie. 

28 years ago broadcast engineer was Peter Brown. 
Thanks to Dadrian HoSang, Sound Engineer at Creative Sounds.
Special thanks to Damion Mitchell.

Pictures were provided by -
(c) The Gleaner Company (Media) Limited 
Mrs. Glynne Manley
Miss Sarah Manley
Mr. Granville Valentine, National Workers' Union
Michael Manley Foundation

I am certain that this interview in its 7 parts will provide new information and insights, generate discussion and passionate debate, and perhaps it will trigger ideas for further, reading, research and investigation.

Thanks for listening. Please share and comment.

Walk good.

Fae

snippet.title: Fidel castro visits jamaica
snippet.description:
FRANCIS: "Castro's visit to Jamaica has rekindled the political turmoil which last year racked this island nation. The debate is over the leadership of the Prime Minister, Michael Manley, and whether or not he is leading Jamaica towards Communism. Manley, who is Castro's closest ally in the Caribbean, has told his people he is building a democratic socialist state but during last year's election that philosophy provoked violence -- one hundred people were killed, it scared away the American tourist, and ruined the country's economy. In a speech yesterday, Castro went out of his way to defend Manley. He said he would never interfere in Jamaica's internal affairs, but at the same time he painted a rosy picture of Communist life in Cuba for his poor Jamaican audience. And he took a slap at the United States."



CASTRO: "The fascist economic blockade, military aggressions, and they forced our country to a lot of sacrifices. Well, our country, ... our people ... in fact people, like this one right in front of us, people like you, who jump, talk, even shoot- and want."



"Castro may give the Jamaicans ideas bit he can't give them what they need the most, money to rebuild the tourism industry."

snippet.title: The hidden truth about Edward Seaga; The US prefered him over Manley.
snippet.description:
Donation:  https://www.paypal.com/donate/?hosted_button_id=6X537T9YSMHRY

 In this video, we're going to explore the hidden truth about Edward Seaga. He was the Prime Minister of Jamaica during some of the most turbulent times in Jamaican history, and he was successful in rebuilding the Jamaican economy after the long lasting rule of Micheal Manley.

Manley was great friends with America's enemy, Fidel Castro, and he wanted to abolish the capitalist system in Jamaica. Edward Seaga, on the other hand, was the man who ultimately succeeded in tying knots with the American government and bringing stability to Jamaica. Seaga was a pragmatic politician who knew how to work with the Americans, and his efforts were crucial in rebuilding the Jamaican economy and restoring peace and order.

Comment, like and Subscribe.
Thanks for supporting my channel.
ENJOY!

outcome.

snippet.title: Tune In To Hear Beverley Manley's conversation with Emprezz Golding
snippet.description:
Beverley Lois Anderson-Manley Duncan (born 8 November 1941) is a Jamaican public figure. In the 1970s she emerged as a leader of women's rights advocacy, leading a campaign for a maternity leave. From 1972 to 1993, she was married to Michael Manley, who was Prime Minister of Jamaica from 1972 to 1980. Manley was known for being more radical than her husband and helping him connect with Jamaica's black population. She was a popular figure among the majority of Jamaicans. Manley also co-founded a radio show with Eric Anthony Abrahams in 1992. After divorcing Michael, she published The Manley Memoirs in 2008, and in 2012 she married Donald Keith Duncan.

#ThisIsHerStory #BeverlyManley #MichaelManley #EmprezzGolding
#EveryWoman #Jamaicannews
#BeverlyManley

snippet.title: You Won't Believe What Micheal Manley Did!
snippet.description:
Donations to support my channel can be sent directly to my Paypal.
https://www.paypal.com/donate/?hosted_button_id=6X537T9YSMHRY

USE HEADSETS OR EARPHONES FOR BETTER AUDIO.

Michael Manley was the Prime Minister of Jamaica from 1976 to 1992. During his time in office, Manley had a contentious relationship with the United States of America. Manley was critical of American foreign policy and the support that the United States gave to political groups opposed to his government. This critical attitude led to strained relations between the two countries.

In this video, we'll tell the story of Michael Manley and his stained friendship with the United States of America. Manley's relationship with the United States of America was a tumultuous one, and his story is a cautionary tale about the power of politics and the importance.

Thanks for Watching and supporting my Channel.
Like, Comment, Subscribe if you enjoyed!

snippet.title: The BEST PERFORMING Jamaican Prime Ministers (Trailblazers of Jamaican Politics)
snippet.description:
Title: "Trailblazers of Jamaican Politics: Unveiling the Best Performing Prime Ministers" 

Introduction:
In the vibrant tapestry of Jamaican politics, there have been extraordinary leaders who have left an indelible mark on the nation's history. Their visionary policies, unwavering dedication, and transformative initiatives have shaped Jamaica's trajectory, lifting its people and positioning the island nation on the global stage. Join us as we delve into the lives and accomplishments of these exceptional prime ministers, whose legacy continues to resonate through the generations.
